categories.introduction: The Fungi Ontology catalogs canonical culinary fungi — edible fungi used as culinary ingredients worldwide. This domain models ingredients, not biological taxonomy.
categories.name: Fungi
categories.seo_description: Explore canonical culinary fungi by group — usage intensity, identity, and pairing metadata in the PairingMethod Food Ontology.
categories.summary: Canonical culinary fungi across four immutable groups: Cultivated Mushrooms, Wild Mushrooms, Truffles, and Specialty Fungi.

fungi.seo_description: Abalone mushroom is a thick-stemmed Pleurotus prized for seafood-like bite — seared with garlic, it pairs with Albariño, Vermentino, and crisp coastal w...
fungi.summary: Abalone mushroom is a thick-stemmed Pleurotus prized for seafood-like bite — seared with garlic, it pairs with Albariño, Vermentino, and crisp coastal whites.

fungi.seo_description: Almond mushroom is a cultivated Agaricus with marzipan-like aroma when cooked — its distinctive scent pairs with aromatic whites and soft, fruit-forward...
fungi.summary: Almond mushroom is a cultivated Agaricus with marzipan-like aroma when cooked — its distinctive scent pairs with aromatic whites and soft, fruit-forward reds.

fungi.seo_description: Bamboo pith is a lacy specialty fungus in Chinese haute cuisine — prized for texture in clear broths and banquet soups that pair with aged Chenin and re...
fungi.summary: Bamboo pith is a lacy specialty fungus in Chinese haute cuisine — prized for texture in clear broths and banquet soups that pair with aged Chenin and refined whites.

fungi.seo_description: Beech mushroom grows in clustered caps with nutty, slightly sweet flavor — Japanese and Nordic preparations suit sake, Pinot Gris, and mineral whites.
fungi.summary: Beech mushroom grows in clustered caps with nutty, slightly sweet flavor — Japanese and Nordic preparations suit sake, Pinot Gris, and mineral whites.

fungi.seo_description: Bianchetto is a spring white truffle with sharp garlic aroma — lighter than Alba truffle, it pairs with Vermentino, Pecorino dishes, and young Sangiovese.
fungi.summary: Bianchetto is a spring white truffle with sharp garlic aroma — lighter than Alba truffle, it pairs with Vermentino, Pecorino dishes, and young Sangiovese.

fungi.seo_description: Black truffle is the benchmark luxury fungus — intense earth, garlic, and chocolate notes over eggs, pasta, and root vegetables demand great age-worthy ...
fungi.summary: Black truffle is the benchmark luxury fungus — intense earth, garlic, and chocolate notes over eggs, pasta, and root vegetables demand great age-worthy reds and structured whites.

fungi.seo_description: Black trumpet is a thin, smoky wild mushroom that concentrates when dried — its deep umami suits Syrah, Rhône blends, and full-bodied southern whites.
fungi.summary: Black trumpet is a thin, smoky wild mushroom that concentrates when dried — its deep umami suits Syrah, Rhône blends, and full-bodied southern whites.

fungi.seo_description: Blewit is a lilac-toned wild mushroom with floral aroma and firm flesh — cream sauces and game birds pair with Pinot Noir and aromatic northern whites.
fungi.summary: Blewit is a lilac-toned wild mushroom with floral aroma and firm flesh — cream sauces and game birds pair with Pinot Noir and aromatic northern whites.

fungi.seo_description: Burgundy truffle bridges summer and black winter truffles in aroma — forest dishes and potato preparations pair with Pinot Noir, Gamay, and earthy Chard...
fungi.summary: Burgundy truffle bridges summer and black winter truffles in aroma — forest dishes and potato preparations pair with Pinot Noir, Gamay, and earthy Chardonnay.

fungi.seo_description: Button mushroom is the world's most common cultivated fungus — mild umami and soft texture anchor everyday sautés, soups, and global comfort dishes that...
fungi.summary: Button mushroom is the world's most common cultivated fungus — mild umami and soft texture anchor everyday sautés, soups, and global comfort dishes that pair with crisp whites and light reds.

fungi.seo_description: Cauliflower mushroom forms frilly, noodle-like clusters with mild almond flavor — butter-braised preparations suit Chardonnay, Albariño, and soft reds.
fungi.summary: Cauliflower mushroom forms frilly, noodle-like clusters with mild almond flavor — butter-braised preparations suit Chardonnay, Albariño, and soft reds.

fungi.seo_description: Chestnut mushroom has a nutty, slightly bitter cap that holds up to roasting — its savory edge suits medium reds and structured Mediterranean whites.
fungi.summary: Chestnut mushroom has a nutty, slightly bitter cap that holds up to roasting — its savory edge suits medium reds and structured Mediterranean whites.

fungi.summary: Chicken of the woods is a shelf-like wild fungus with poultry-like texture when cooked — fried or braised, it pairs with unoaked whites and medium-bodied reds.

fungi.seo_description: Cinnamon cap is a brick-red cultivated mushroom with nutty flavor when young — sautéed applications match earthy whites and light Italian reds.
fungi.summary: Cinnamon cap is a brick-red cultivated mushroom with nutty flavor when young — sautéed applications match earthy whites and light Italian reds.

fungi.summary: Cremini is a mature brown-capped Agaricus with deeper flavor than button mushroom — roasted or braised, it adds savory depth that suits medium-bodied reds and earthy whites.

fungi.seo_description: Elm oyster is a cultivated Pleurotus relative with firm white caps — mild umami in stir-fries and soups suits crisp whites and light-bodied reds.
fungi.summary: Elm oyster is a cultivated Pleurotus relative with firm white caps — mild umami in stir-fries and soups suits crisp whites and light-bodied reds.

fungi.summary: Enoki brings crisp, mild mushrooms to hot pots, noodles, and salads — subtle flavor pairs with sparkling wine, sake-style whites, and delicate seafood dishes.

fungi.summary: Fried chicken mushroom is a clustered specialty fungus with mild, poultry-like flavor — East Asian braises and hot pots suit aromatic whites and light reds.

fungi.seo_description: Hedgehog mushroom has a sweet, nutty profile and tooth-like spines instead of gills — sautéed with herbs, it pairs with Pinot Gris, Chardonnay, and Gamay.
fungi.summary: Hedgehog mushroom has a sweet, nutty profile and tooth-like spines instead of gills — sautéed with herbs, it pairs with Pinot Gris, Chardonnay, and Gamay.

fungi.summary: King oyster offers a thick stem and firm, scallop-like texture when seared — its clean umami suits full-bodied whites, rosé, and soft tannins.

fungi.summary: Lobster mushroom is a bright orange parasitic fungus with seafood-like sweetness — its color and flavor suit rosé, Viognier, and shellfish-friendly whites.

fungi.summary: Maitake is a frilled cultivated fungus with deep forest umami — roasted or pan-fried, it matches earthy Pinot, Nebbiolo, and barrel-fermented whites.

fungi.summary: Matsutake is a rare pine-scented wild mushroom central to Japanese kaiseki — its spicy-cinnamon aroma pairs with sake, aged Riesling, and restrained Pinot Noir.

fungi.summary: Morel is a honeycombed spring wild mushroom with intense earthy-nutty flavor — butter sauces and poultry pair with aged whites, Champagne, and elegant reds.

fungi.summary: Nameko is a small Japanese specialty fungus with viscous cap and nutty aroma — miso soup and soba pair with sake, Grüner Veltliner, and light, umami-friendly whites.

fungi.summary: Parasol mushroom is a large wild cap with hazelnut notes when breaded or grilled — Central European preparations pair with Grüner Veltliner, Riesling, and lager-style whites.

fungi.summary: Pioppino offers nutty, slightly sweet caps on clustered stems — Italian and Asian sautés pair with Sangiovese, Barbera, and mineral-driven whites.

fungi.summary: Shaggy mane is a delicate wild mushroom best eaten young — mild flavor in egg dishes and soups pairs with Sauvignon Blanc and light, crisp whites.

fungi.summary: Wine cap is a large, wine-stained cultivated mushroom with firm texture — grilled or stewed, it pairs with Gamay, Pinot Noir, and rustic country reds.

fungi.summary: Wood ear is a gelatinous specialty fungus valued for crunch in Chinese hot pot and stir-fries — neutral flavor carries sauce pairings toward Riesling, Gewürztraminer, and off-dry whites.

groups.introduction: Cultivated mushrooms in the Fungi Ontology — commercially farmed culinary fungi including button, cremini, portobello, shiitake, and oyster mushrooms.
groups.name: Cultivated Mushrooms
groups.seo_description: Canonical cultivated mushrooms — button, shiitake, oyster, and related culinary fungi in the PairingMethod Fungi Ontology.
groups.summary: Cultivated Mushrooms — 15 canonical fungi in the Fungi Ontology.

groups.introduction: Wild mushrooms in the Fungi Ontology — foraged and seasonal culinary fungi including chanterelle, morel, porcini, and matsutake.
groups.name: Wild Mushrooms
groups.seo_description: Canonical wild mushrooms — chanterelle, morel, porcini, and related foraged fungi in the PairingMethod Fungi Ontology.
groups.summary: Wild Mushrooms — 14 canonical fungi in the Fungi Ontology.

groups.introduction: Truffles in the Fungi Ontology — luxury culinary fungi including black and white truffle.
groups.name: Truffles
groups.seo_description: Canonical truffles — black truffle, white truffle, and related luxury fungi in the PairingMethod Fungi Ontology.
groups.summary: Truffles — 5 canonical fungi in the Fungi Ontology.

groups.introduction: Specialty fungi in the Fungi Ontology — distinctive culinary fungi including wood ear, lion's mane, beech mushroom, and nameko.
groups.name: Specialty Fungi
groups.seo_description: Canonical specialty fungi — wood ear, lion's mane, nameko, and related culinary fungi in the PairingMethod Fungi Ontology.
groups.summary: Specialty Fungi — 9 canonical fungi in the Fungi Ontology.

meta.description: Fungi Ontology — canonical catalog populated (FOOD-06B). Intrinsic metadata only; relationships deferred to FOOD-06D/E.

aroma_profile.description: Reserved aroma characteristic tags — vocabulary frozen in FOOD-06A; do not populate until post-FOOD-06B curation standards.

canonical_culinary_groups.description: Immutable group names and slugs — do not rename or add peer groups in v1.

culinary_group.description: Canonical Culinary Group value on leaf entities — aligns to immutable group slugs.

flavor_profile.description: Reserved flavor characteristic tags — vocabulary frozen in FOOD-06A; do not populate until post-FOOD-06B curation standards.

texture_profile.description: Reserved texture characteristic tags — vocabulary frozen in FOOD-06A; do not populate until post-FOOD-06B curation standards.

usage_intensity.description: Intrinsic culinary prominence in dish composition — not Pairing Engine reasoning.

domain_exclusions.note: Entities in these domains are cross-referenced by canonical ID only — never duplicated intrinsically.
domain_exclusions.protein_mushrooms_legacy: Protein Foods mushrooms group — canonical ownership transfers to Fungi; ID migration deferred to FOOD-14

fungi_category.description: Top-level fungi domain hub (single category in v1).

fungi_group.description: Canonical Culinary Group (Cultivated Mushrooms, Wild Mushrooms, etc.).

fungus.description: Canonical culinary fungus entity with frozen intrinsic metadata.

schema.taxonomy_note: Groups are culinary, not mycological. See docs/FUNGI_GOVERNANCE.md.
